summary: The primary pattern for access canal distribution is a single root canal; however, up to approximately one-third may have an additional mesially located second canal making it more common with dual configurations observed as well. To ensure comprehensive treatment planning involving these structures requires careful consideration based upon imaging modalities such as CBCT scans which provide detailed visualization necessary during endodontic therapy management decisions specifically tailored per patient needs due variable anatomical presentations noted clinically across individuals' teeth sets within population samples
